page: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The configuration delivers 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, backed by dedicated CPU allocation. Memory resources consist of 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores. Memory bandwidth read-only small block operations reach top-tier levels at 9051.8 GB/sec, while large block read and write operations maintain strong performance. In database-related workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier efficiency with 136.38 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and strong performance in PassMark database operations. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, ranges from average to strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.32xlarge offers a high-density memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of dedicated local storage. The lack of local NVMe storage and hardware GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage solutions for persistent data and limiting its suitability for heavy GPU-bound machine learning tasks. However, its massive memory capacity and high-throughput network interface make it highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz, delivering 128 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical cores. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high efficiency in database operations, including 136.38 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. This hardware profile makes the r6a.32xlarge highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-heavy data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor, designed for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring one terabyte of RAM and one hundred twenty-eight virtual cores for demanding enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 at 3.6 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
